description: Create a complete workflow end-to-end: name, trigger, eligible records, settings, actions, and publish
preconditions: User has Edit workflows permission, or Super Admin
Account has Professional or Enterprise tier of an applicable hub
All custom properties referenced by triggers and actions already exist
External integrations referenced by actions (e.g., Slack) are connected

execution_methods.preference_reason: HubSpot's workflow API does not support all action types we need. UI is the only complete method.

url_parameters.host_subdomain: Varies by account region — observed 'app-na2' for North America 2. Read from HUBSPOT_HOST env var.

success_signal.left_panel_heading: Choose a trigger to start this workflow

steps.intent: [Phase 1: Name] Click the workflow name at the top of the canvas and rename it

element_to_find.visual_description: Centered title at the top of the canvas, shown as 'Unnamed workflow' until set

steps.intent: [Phase 2: Trigger] Select 'Met filter criteria' as the trigger type from the trigger picker (already open by default)

element_to_find.visual_description: Card in the left-side trigger picker, alongside 'Trigger manually' and 'On a schedule'

expected_outcome.panel_shows: Choose a type of record that can enroll

steps.expansion_scope_not_covered: Other trigger types (Trigger manually, On a schedule, Data values, Emails/calls, Websites/media, Automations triggered, Custom events) require separate documentation

steps.intent: [Phase 3: Eligible records] Choose the record type that can enroll

element_to_find.visual_description: Vertical list of record types: Contact, Company, Deal, Ticket, Call, Cart, etc.

expected_outcome.selected_record_type_appears: Selected type shows in the heading 'Only enroll records that meet these conditions'

steps.intent: [Phase 3: Eligible records] Click the 'Add criteria' button to open the filter category picker

element_to_find.visual_description: Button below 'This workflow doesn't have any...' text. Note: this is the FIRST 'Add criteria' button — opens the category picker

steps.intent: [Phase 3: Eligible records] Select filter category — 'Contact properties' for the POC. This appears under 'Object information'.

element_to_find.visual_description: Listed under the 'Object information' section in the Add criteria panel

steps.expansion_scope_not_covered: Other filter categories: Segment (List) membership, Workflow enrollment, Import membership, Marketing interactions, Ads interactions, etc. Only Contact properties documented for POC.

steps.intent: [Phase 3: Eligible records] Search for and select the trigger property
[Phase 3: Eligible records] Verify or change the operator (defaulted based on property type)

element_to_find.position: Middle field of the criteria row

steps.notes: The operator defaults based on the property's data type. Only change if the default doesn't match the requirement.

steps.intent: [Phase 3: Eligible records] Enter the comparison value

element_to_find.position: Third (bottom) field of the criteria row

steps.intent: [Phase 3: Eligible records] (Optional) Add additional criteria within the same group (AND logic) or in a new group (OR logic)

branches.additional_AND_condition: Click the 'Add criteria' button inside Group 1 (positioned after the 'and' text). Repeat steps 5–8.
branches.additional_OR_group: Click the 'or' link/button outside Group 1, then the adjacent 'Add criteria' button to create Group 2. Repeat steps 5–8.

steps.notes: Distinguishing the two 'Add criteria' buttons: the one INSIDE a group with 'and' label adds an AND condition to that group; the one OUTSIDE groups (next to 'or') creates a new OR group.

steps.intent: [Phase 3: Eligible records] Click Next to advance to Settings

element_to_find.context_text: Allow contacts to re-enroll after completing the workflow

steps.intent: [Phase 4: Settings] Click 'Save and continue' to finalize trigger setup and open the action picker
[Phase 5: Actions] For each action in plan.actions, configure it via the action picker

loop_sub_steps.intent: If this is NOT the first action, click the '+' button on the canvas below the previous node to open the action picker

element_to_find.visual_description: Plus (+) button on canvas, between trigger card and any existing action nodes, or below the last action node

set_property_value.action_description_in_picker: Edit, copy, and clear CRM property values

loop_sub_steps.expansion_scope_not_covered: Other actions: Delay, Branch, Create task, Send internal email notification, Send Slack notification, Create record. Each requires its own branch in this conditional with specific config_sub_steps. Start with set_property_value only.

loop_sub_steps.intent: Configure 'Edit record' action — verify Record type
loop_sub_steps.notes: Defaults to the current workflow's record type. Usually no change needed.

loop_sub_steps.intent: Configure 'Edit record' action — select property to edit
Configure 'Edit record' action — select change type
Configure 'Edit record' action — choose value (only when change_type == Replace)
loop_sub_steps.notes: Input shape depends on the property's field type. Dropdown property shows option picker. Number property shows numeric input. Text property shows textbox.

loop_sub_steps.intent: Click Save to commit this action

element_to_find.visual_description: Top right of action configuration panel

steps.intent: [Phase 6: Publish] Click 'Review and Turn On' button to open the review sidebar

element_to_find.visual_description: Top right of canvas, dark background button (becomes the primary action once the workflow has a valid trigger and at least one action)

expected_outcome.loading_state: Sidebar may show loading state briefly while it calculates current enrollment count

steps.intent: [Phase 6: Publish] Wait for the enrollment criteria count to finish loading

element_to_find.visual_description: Highlighted info card at top of Step 1 showing the count of contacts that currently match

steps.notes: The number is informational only — does not need to be captured. Just confirms the sidebar has finished its initial load.

steps.intent: [Phase 6: Publish] Choose enrollment behavior for existing records that already match

element_to_find.question_text: Do you want to enroll contacts that currently meet the enrollment criteria when the workflow turns on?

options.label: Yes, enroll contacts who meet the enrollment criteria immediately when the workflow is turned on
No, only enroll contacts who meet the enrollment criteria after the workflow is turned on

value_mapping.true: Yes, enroll contacts who meet the enrollment criteria immediately when the workflow is turned on
value_mapping.false: No, only enroll contacts who meet the enrollment criteria after the workflow is turned on

steps.notes: Defaults to 'No' on first open. For most production workflows the safer choice is 'No' — enrolling existing records can trigger mass action sequences unintentionally. For risk-status workflows, 'Yes' may be desired to score the existing database immediately.

steps.intent: [Phase 6: Publish] Skip the remaining review steps and proceed to activation

element_to_find.visual_description: Bottom-left of the Review Workflow sidebar

steps.expansion_scope_not_covered: Clicking 'Next' instead would walk through Steps 2, 3, 4+ of the Review Workflow sidebar (additional settings like suppression lists, association settings, etc.). For POC we skip to the end. Document these intermediate review steps if needed later.

steps.intent: [Phase 6: Publish] Click 'Turn On Workflow' to activate

element_to_find.visual_description: Final button in the Review Workflow sidebar after skipping

secondary_check.rationale: Button label flips from 'Review and Turn On' / 'Turn On Workflow' to 'Turn off' only when activation succeeds

fallback_check.expected: Workflow appears in list with active/ON status indicator

known_failures.symptom: 'Save and continue' button is disabled at Settings step
known_failures.likely_cause: Trigger criteria not fully configured (missing value in a filter row)
known_failures.recovery: Navigate back to Eligible records step, complete any incomplete criteria rows, retry

known_failures.symptom: Action category panel doesn't show expected action
known_failures.likely_cause: Action requires an integration (e.g., Slack) that isn't connected, OR the action belongs to a different category than expected
known_failures.recovery: Verify integration is connected in Settings → Integrations. Check action category mapping in workflow capability entry.

known_failures.symptom: Trigger property dropdown doesn't contain expected property
known_failures.likely_cause: Custom property hasn't been created yet on this object
known_failures.recovery: Verify property exists via inspector tool. If missing, execute create_custom_property operation first, then return to this step.

known_failures.symptom: Two 'Add criteria' buttons visible — agent clicks the wrong one
known_failures.likely_cause: Confusion between intra-group AND criteria button and inter-group OR criteria button
known_failures.recovery: Use position-based disambiguation: the button INSIDE the Group 1 card (after the 'and' text) adds AND criteria. The button OUTSIDE groups (next to 'or' text) creates a new OR group.

known_failures.symptom: 'Review and Turn On' button is disabled
known_failures.likely_cause: Workflow has invalid state — missing trigger, missing actions, or actions with unfilled required fields
known_failures.recovery: Inspect canvas for action nodes with warning indicators (yellow/red badges). Complete any partial configurations before retrying.

known_failures.symptom: Review Workflow sidebar shows '0 contacts meet the enrollment criteria' and consultant expected non-zero
known_failures.likely_cause: Trigger conditions are too restrictive, source property is unpopulated for existing contacts, or property values don't actually match the operator/value combination
known_failures.recovery: Surface as informational to consultant — workflow can still activate, but no existing contacts will be processed. May indicate a planning gap (e.g., the 22% null data scenario).

known_failures.symptom: 'Turn On Workflow' click succeeds but status doesn't change to ON
known_failures.likely_cause: Backend save failed silently, or page state stale
known_failures.recovery: Wait 5 seconds, refresh page, check workflow list. If still off, re-attempt publish from canvas. If repeated failure, surface as backend error.

expansion_scope_not_covered: Communications actions (Send internal email, Send Slack notification)
CRM actions beyond Edit record (Create task, Create record, etc.)
Event-based, scheduled, webhook-based triggers (only filter-based with Contact properties documented)
Filter categories beyond Contact properties (Segment membership, Workflow enrollment, Marketing interactions, etc.)
Intermediate Review Workflow sidebar steps beyond Step 1: Enrollment (Steps 2, 3, 4+ that 'Skip to Turn On' bypasses — includes suppression lists, association settings, performance goals)
Manual one-off enrollment via the 'Enroll' button that appears post-activation
